summary: Import-time machine-bound AES-decrypted code execution disguised as a TypeScript linter
details: On require() of this package, index.js silently loads lib/perf.js inside a try/catch that swallows errors. lib/perf.js reads /etc/machine-id, derives an AES-128-CBC key from its bytes (with a hardcoded IV), decrypts an embedded ~10KB hex blob, and executes the decrypted plaintext via `new Function(r)()`. The machine-id-derived key restricts decryption to a specific target host, preventing reviewers from inspecting what actually runs. The package's advertised purpose is a TypeScript naming-convention linter — index.js contains a trivial regex linter, while lib/perf.js (the only other shipped file) contains no perf or lint logic and exists solely to perform the keyed dynamic-code-execution. The cover-story shape (silent require + error suppression of the hidden module, advertised purpose unrelated to crypto/exec) combined with import-time machine-bound `Function()` execution of an opaque blob is unambiguous malicious dropper behavior. Any developer who requires this package on the targeted host executes attacker-controlled code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
